ai.description: The html element is missing the lang attribute, which prevents screen readers from correctly pronouncing the content in the appropriate language.
The link text "Click here" does not describe the destination or purpose of the link, forcing screen reader users to navigate out of context to understand where the link leads.
The link has an empty aria-label attribute which overrides the visible text, resulting in no accessible name for screen reader users.
The button with text "Menu" controls a navigation menu but lacks aria-expanded attribute to indicate the current state (expanded/collapsed) to screen reader users.
The logo image lacks an alt attribute, preventing screen reader users from understanding that this is the company logo.
Multiple section elements exist on the page without distinguishing labels, making it difficult for screen reader users to navigate between regions.
The heading hierarchy skips from h2 to h4, bypassing h3, which disrupts the logical outline of the page for screen reader users.
The fieldset groups form controls but lacks a legend element, failing to provide a programmatic label for the group to screen reader users.
The email input field has no associated label, placeholder is not a sufficient accessible name, preventing screen reader users from knowing what information to enter.
The email input collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with filling in their information.
The text input for full name has no associated label, making it impossible for screen reader users to identify the field's purpose.
The input for full name collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete attribute, reducing usability for users with cognitive disabilities.
The telephone input field lacks a label, preventing screen reader users from understanding what data is required.
The telephone input collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from autofill assistance.
The select dropdown has no label, leaving screen reader users unaware of what option they are selecting.
The textarea for messages has no associated label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.

ai.issueType: Table header cell not marked as th
ai.description: The cell containing "Plan" functions as a column header but is marked as td instead of th, preventing screen readers from identifying it as a header.

ai.issueType: Missing language attribute on html element
ai.description: The <html> element lacks a lang attribute, which prevents screen readers from identifying the page's primary language. This affects users who rely on correct pronunciation and language-specific processing.

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
lighthouse.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

lighthouse.issueType: `<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute
lighthouse.description: If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. [Learn more about the `lang` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/html-has-lang).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Select elements do not have associated label elements.
lighthouse.description: Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. [Learn more about the `select` element](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/select-name).

lighthouse.issueType: Document does not have a main landmark.
lighthouse.description: One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. [Learn more about landmarks](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/landmark-one-main).

lighthouse.issueType: `<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers.
lighthouse.description: Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. [Learn more about table headers](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/td-has-header).
